title: eriogonum umbellatum var. ellipticum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. ellipticum, also known as Sulfur Buckwheat or Sulfur-flower Buckwheat, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum var. ellipticum include the potential treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant may include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
